title: Curaleaf Q3 Revenue Falls 3%
text: Curaleaf Holdings reported third-quarter 2025 financial results showing net revenue of $320.2 million, representing a 3% year-over-year decline from $330.5 million in Q3 2024. However, the company achieved sequential growth of 2% compared to Q2 2025. Chairman and CEO Boris Jordan noted that price compression continued to be a headwind, though the domestic segment remained stable while the international division delivered strong performance with 12% sequential growth and 56% year-over-year growth. Key financial metrics include: Adjusted gross profit margin improved to 50%, up 115 basis points year-over-year, Adjusted EBITDA reached $69.3 million with a 21.6% margin, Operating cash flow from continuing operations of $53 million for Q3, Free cash flow from continuing operations of $37 million for Q3, and Cash position at quarter-end of $107.5 million. Jordan highlighted progress from the company's Return to Our Roots initiative, emphasizing improved product quality, margin expansion, and cash flow optimization despite ongoing macroeconomic pressures.
